He catches a falling data chip mid-air, servo fingers snapping shut with mechanical precision.
He crushes a surveillance drone in his fist, sparks and metal fragments scattering.
He reaches toward a flickering holographic interface, cybernetic hand passing through blue light.
He draws a plasma pistol from his thigh holster, barrel already tracking a target.
He shoulders through a reinforced steel door, hydraulics whining under the strain.
He plugs a wrist cable into a terminal port, streams of data flooding his optics.
He rips open a wall panel with both hands, exposing tangled wires and circuits.
He examines a blood-stained knife, rotating it slowly between articulated metal fingers.
He deflects incoming shrapnel with his forearm, sparks showering off reinforced plating.
He lights a cigarette with a finger-mounted plasma igniter, face glowing orange.
He leans against a concrete pillar, optical sensors scanning the shadowed perimeter beyond.
He braces against an explosion's shockwave, coat whipping as debris rushes past him.
He intercepts a thrown blade bare-handed, servo grip halting it centimeters from his face.
He pries open his forearm maintenance hatch, recalibrating exposed circuitry with precise movements.
He extracts a memory core from a dead operative's skull jack, wires trailing.
He holsters his sidearm after confirming the kill, motion smooth and practiced.
He yanks a security officer forward by the collar, lifting him onto his toes.
He inserts a override key into a blast door mechanism, servos twisting with force.
He turns sharply on his heel, weapon raised to cover the corridor behind him.
He snaps a guard's baton in half with one hand, fragments clattering to the floor.
He wipes blood from his knuckles onto his coat, expression cold and unchanging.
He strides through a laser grid, red beams tracing across his armored frame.
He presses his palm against a biometric scanner, forcing the locked system to comply.
He reloads his weapon with mechanical efficiency, spent magazine hitting the ground mid-motion.
He drags a body backward with one hand, leaving a dark smear across the floor.
